Operation 'applyMassiveAction' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'Apply massive action' is too generic and doesn't convey the bulk/batch nature of the operation. Description lacks critical details about inputs (itemtype parameter, request body structure), outputs (BulkResult schema), and side effects (state changes to items). Enhanced version clarifies that this is a bulk operation, specifies the itemtype path parameter, explains the request body structure, identifies the return type (BulkResult), and documents that items are modified. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation (bulk modifications) and what to expect.

data.current_description: Run a massive action on selected items.
data.summary_suggestion: Execute bulk action on multiple items
data.description_suggestion: Executes a massive action (bulk operation) on multiple selected items of a specified type. Accepts the item type as a path parameter and action details in the request body, including the action name, item IDs, and action-specific parameters. Returns a BulkResult object containing the status and results of the operation for each item. Modifies the state of affected items according to the action performed (e.g., status changes, field updates, deletions).
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Apply massive action' is too generic and doesn't convey the bulk/batch nature of the operation. Description lacks critical details about inputs (itemtype parameter, request body structure), outputs (BulkResult schema), and side effects (state changes to items). Enhanced version clarifies that this is a bulk operation, specifies the itemtype path parameter, explains the request body structure, identifies the return type (BulkResult), and documents that items are modified. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation (bulk modifications) and what to expect.

message: Operation 'killSession' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks detail about side effects (token invalidation) and the consequence for subsequent API calls. Enhanced description clarifies that the token becomes invalid and explains the impact on future requests for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Destroy the current session identified by the session token.
data.description_suggestion: Terminate the current authenticated session and invalidate the session token. After calling this endpoint, the session token will no longer be valid for subsequent API requests. Requires an active session token.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description lacks detail about side effects (token invalidation) and the consequence for subsequent API calls. Enhanced description clarifies that the token becomes invalid and explains the impact on future requests for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'lostPassword' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is wordy and unclear about the two distinct flows. Description lacks clarity on required inputs for each scenario, expected outputs, and authentication requirements. Enhanced version distinguishes between recovery initiation and reset flows, specifies required parameters for each, clarifies return value, and notes lack of authentication requirement for AI agent clarity.

data.current_summary: Request password recovery or reset password
data.current_description: Send a password recovery email or reset a password using a token.
data.description_suggestion: Initiate a password recovery process by sending a recovery email to the user, or reset a password using a valid recovery token. When initiating recovery, provide the user's email or login. When resetting, provide the recovery token and new password. Returns confirmation of the action performed. No authentication required for this operation.
data.reasoning: Original summary is wordy and unclear about the two distinct flows. Description lacks clarity on required inputs for each scenario, expected outputs, and authentication requirements. Enhanced version distinguishes between recovery initiation and reset flows, specifies required parameters for each, clarifies return value, and notes lack of authentication requirement for AI agent clarity.

data.description_suggestion: Switch the authenticated user's active profile to the specified profile. Requires the target profile ID in the request body. The new profile must be one of the user's assigned profiles. Returns the updated active profile object. This change affects the user's permissions and access rights for subsequent API requests.

data.current_description: Run a massive action on selected items.
data.summary_suggestion: Execute bulk action on multiple items
data.description_suggestion: Executes a massive action (bulk operation) on multiple selected items of a specified type. Accepts the item type as a path parameter and action details in the request body, including the action name, item IDs, and action-specific parameters. Returns a BulkResult object containing the status and results of the operation for each item. Modifies the state of affected items according to the action performed (e.g., status changes, field updates, deletions).

Returns confirmation of the operation status.

data.current_description: Return a collection of sub-items for the identified item.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a collection of sub-items (child items) associated with a parent item of the specified itemtype. Requires itemtype, parent item ID, and subItemtype as path parameters. Supports pagination and filtering through query parameters. Returns a SearchResult containing matching sub-items with their properties. Useful for exploring hierarchical relationships between items (e.g., components of a computer, tickets related to a user).

Returns a MassiveAction collection describing each available action, including action identifiers, labels, and applicable parameters. Use this to discover what bulk operations are supported before executing massive actions on multiple items.
